Title: Zahid proposes Orang Asli officer at Negri Sembilan MB office
Article:
JELUBU: A special officer representing the Orang Asli community could be appointed at the Negri Sembilan Menteri Besar's Office without the need for a manifesto pledge, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
He said the proposal was raised by the Orang Asli community during his visit to Kampung Orang Asli Tohor here today and described the request as reasonable.
"That does not require a manifesto. It can be implemented administratively.
"This is their request and I think it is a reasonable one because such positions have already been established in Pahang and Perak," he told reporters after the visit.
Also present were Datuk Dr Ramli Mohd Nor, Chennah candidate Siow Kong Choon, Pertang candidate Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias, and Umno secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki.
Zahid, who is also Barisan Nasional chairman, said appointing a special officer would strengthen direct engagement between Orang Asli leaders and the Menteri Besar's Office.
He said the arrangement had worked well in Pahang and Perak, where the community had a dedicated representative to channel issues and concerns directly to the state leadership.
"I have also suggested that Johor consider appointing a special officer representing the Orang Asli community at the Menteri Besar's Office because the state has a sizeable Orang Asli population," he said.
Zahid said the appointment could be carried out administratively if BN forms the next Negri Sembilan government.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid: BN-PN understanding needs time before expanding to other polls
Article:
JELEBU: Barisan Nasional's electoral understanding with Perikatan Nasional in Negri Sembilan should be allowed to develop organically before being expanded to other state elections, said Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
The BN chairman said any decision to expand the cooperation would depend on the outcome of the Negri Sembilan election first.
He said the understanding between the two coalitions was still in its early stages and should be allowed to mature naturally based on sincerity and mutual understanding.
"We will first see what happens in Negri Sembilan and after that we will look at what happens in Melaka.
"I hope this compatibility can develop organically and, most importantly, with sincerity.
"Insya-Allah, we will see the results on Aug 1. Not only that, we will also try it in Melaka and several other places if it is suitable," he told reporters after visiting Kampung Orang Asli Tohor here today.
Zahid, who is also deputy prime minister, was responding to a question on PN election director Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor's recent remarks that the BN-PN understanding in Negri Sembilan had the potential to be extended to Putrajaya.
Zahid said while there was room to strengthen cooperation, both sides still needed time to improve coordination on the ground.
"The understanding has only been in place for about a week. Naturally, compatibility has to be built and there are still some sticking points that need to be resolved.
"So, give them the opportunity to resolve those issues," he said.
Asked whether he was satisfied with the current campaign momentum, Zahid said there was still room for improvement despite encouraging progress.
"I think many things can still be improved and there is more work to be done to strengthen our togetherness.
"Since we had previously intended to work together but it was delayed, those past experiences should teach us how to do it better this time," he said.
Zahid said BN and PN campaign workers had received a positive response from voters despite the absence of a formal agreement between the two coalitions.
He said both sides had instead forged an electoral understanding that had worked well during the first week of campaigning and expressed confidence that it would continue until polling day.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man jailed 60 years, ordered 60 strokes of rotan over daughter rape case
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A 56-year-old Indonesian man was sentenced by the Sessions Court today to 60 years' imprisonment and 60 strokes of the rotan after pleading guilty to six counts of raping his biological daughter, resulting in her giving birth to a baby girl.
Judge Mazuliana Abdul Rashid ordered the man to serve 10 years' imprisonment and receive 10 strokes of the rotan for each charge, with the jail terms to run consecutively from the date of his arrest yesterday.
He was charged with raping his daughter, whose relationship to him is such that he is not permitted under the law, religion, custom or usage to marry her.
The court heard that the victim, who is now 19, was first raped in October 2021 and later gave birth to her daughter, who is now four.
A DNA test confirmed that the accused was the baby's biological father.
He also raped the victim on five other occasions in February, March, May and June this year.
The offences were committed at a condominium unit in Taman Midah.
The charges were framed under Section 376(3) of the Penal Code, which carries a jail term of not less than eight years and up to 30 years, and not less than 10 strokes of the rotan upon conviction.
Deputy Public Prosecutor J. Banusha asked the court to impose a heavy sentence, saying the accused had betrayed the trust placed in him as a father and caregiver.
"The repeated assaults had left the victim suffering from severe depression and suicidal thoughts, but she persevered for the sake of her daughter," she said.
The man, who was unrepresented, pleaded for a lighter sentence, saying he had lived here for more than 40 years.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Oil nears US$100 a barrel as Middle East risks deepen
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Renewed tensions in the Middle East have pushed Brent crude prices back towards US$100 a barrel, with markets pricing in growing risks of prolonged supply disruptions and tighter global energy flows.
OCBC said Brent futures are now trading above its forecasts of US$75 a barrel in 4Q26 and US$71 a barrel by mid-2027, after briefly retreating towards pre-conflict levels near US$70 a barrel.
The futures curve has steepened, volatility has increased and tanker movements through the Strait of Hormuz have fallen sharply as security concerns escalate, its commodity strategist Sim Moh Siong said.
The renewed conflict has disrupted oil shipping routes, with a fresh US blockade of Iranian ports adding pressure on exports and delaying expectations of supply normalisation, Sim added.
The situation remains unsettled, with continued US-Iran strikes and concerns that the conflict could widen after Houthi forces reportedly targeted two oil tankers in the Red Sea.
The dispute over shipping rights through the Strait of Hormuz remains unresolved, keeping markets focused on the risk of further attacks on energy infrastructure.
Sim said while crude prices have risen, refined products such as gasoline and diesel have climbed even faster, posing greater risks to consumers and inflation.
Reduced flows through Hormuz, combined with disruptions at Russian refineries, have tightened diesel supplies and pushed up refining margins.
He added that oil prices could ease quickly if diplomatic efforts restore safe passage through key shipping routes.
However, further attacks on tankers or energy facilities could keep Brent above US$100 a barrel.
In response, Gulf producers are accelerating efforts to diversify export routes.
Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates are expanding infrastructure that bypasses Hormuz, while countries such as Kuwait, Qatar and Bahrain remain more vulnerable due to limited alternatives.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PSD introduces Touch 'n Go e-Wallet pension payments, expands MyPesara Plus
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: A total of 835,146 pensioners and derivative pension beneficiaries will benefit from two new Public Service Department (PSD) initiatives, namely pension payment channels through the Touch 'n Go e-Wallet and expanded MyPesara Plus benefits.
Public Service director-general Tan Sri Wan Ahmad Dahlan Abdul Aziz said the alternative payment channel was the first initiative of its kind introduced in the public sector to assist retirees who face difficulties receiving their pensions due to frozen or blocked bank accounts after falling victim to financial scams.
He said the initiative, developed through collaboration between the PSD, the Ministry of Finance, Retirement Fund (Incorporated) (KWAP) and Touch 'n Go e-Wallet, would ensure retirees continue to receive their pension payments without disruption through a more flexible and secure channel.
"By May 2026, a total of 122 pensioners had received their pension payments through this method, involving monthly payments amounting to RM254,841.21," he said in a statement today.
Meanwhile, Wan Ahmad Dahlan said the MyPesara Plus initiative expanded the Pensioner Benefits Programme by transforming the pensioner card into more than just an identification document, allowing retirees to enjoy various benefits through strategic partnerships with the private sector.
He said the initiative would allow retirees to enjoy special offers and discounts from strategic partners in sectors including healthcare, pharmacy, transport, retail, accommodation and various other services through the physical pensioner card or the MyPesara app.
Wan Ahmad Dahlan said the introduction of the two initiatives reflected the PSD's commitment to strengthening the welfare ecosystem for retirees through an innovative, inclusive and technology-driven approach.
"The PSD is not merely responsible for managing retirement matters and pension payments; it is also committed to ensuring retirees continue to enjoy a better quality of life after retirement through services that are more accessible, secure and value-adding," he said, adding that the department would continue expanding partnerships with ministries, agencies and industry partners to provide more benefits for retirees.
"Appreciating the contributions of civil servants does not end on the day they retire. Instead, it continues through the government's ongoing efforts to ensure they receive quality services and benefits that remain relevant to their post-retirement needs," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Asia's tech exports cushioned from tariff blow
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Asia's technology exporters are expected to weather the latest round of the US tariffs better than many other sectors, as exemptions for semiconductors and other strategic products help shield the region from a sharper trade shock.
According to Moody's Analytics, the artificial intelligence (AI) boom has also provided an important buffer, with strong demand for chips and technology-related products offsetting some of the headwinds from higher tariffs and geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.
The United States has imposed new tariffs ranging from 10 per cent to 12.5 per cent on imports from 60 trading partners under Section 301 of the Trade Act of 1974 effective today, replacing the temporary 10 per cent tariff that expired on July 24.
The measures follow investigations by the Office of the US Trade Representative (USTR), which found the affected economies had not sufficiently prohibited or enforced bans on imports made with forced labour.
Malaysia is among 17 economies placed in the lower 10 per cent tariff category, alongside Indonesia, India, Canada, Mexico and the United Kingdom.
The USTR said the lower rate applies to economies that have introduced forced labour import bans, made commitments through trade agreements or have partially implemented enforcement measures.
Other economies, including China, Singapore and Australia, face tariffs of 12.5 per cent, while Japan and South Korea are also subject to 12.5 per cent tariffs, with some relief available under existing trade agreements.
Moody's Analytics said China's effective tariff rate rises to 23.4 per cent, up from 21.3 per cent under the temporary Section 122 regime, although it remains below the level before the US Supreme Court ruling earlier this year.
The firm noted that, overall, the latest tariff regime is less punitive for several Asian economies than before the court invalidated tariffs imposed under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act.
Moody's Analytics said further Section 301 investigations into excess industrial capacity remain underway, meaning additional tariff announcements are possible.
It added that while Chinese President Xi Jinping's expected visit to Washington in September could support further trade negotiations, the latest measures reinforce that tariffs are likely to remain a structural feature of US trade policy.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: K9 unit joins probe into Selangor Wholesale Market fire [WATCH]
Article:
SERDANG: The Fire and Rescue Department's K9 unit was deployed to the Selangor Wholesale Market as forensic officers began investigating the fire that destroyed about 90 per cent of the premises.
The department's trained detection dogs, Rusty and Sam, arrived at the scene in a special vehicle, each wearing protective boots to protect their paws as they navigated the debris-strewn site.
The two dogs, a Cocker Spaniel and a Springer Spaniel, then began combing through the charred remains, assisting forensic officers in their search for clues to determine how the blaze started.
Earlier, Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ashrul Riezal Asbar said an excavator arrived at about noon to clear debris.
This was to allow the department's forensic team to begin its investigation.
Ashrul said the fire was fully extinguished at 6.20am after firefighters worked through the night to contain the blaze.
He had earlier said the cause of the fire was expected to be determined within 14 days.
No casualties were reported in the blaze.
The emergency call was received at 11.04pm last night before the fire was brought under control at 1.20am.
The operation involved 68 fire and rescue personnel, five Fire Rescue Tenders, one Light Fire Rescue Tender, two Aerial Ladder Platforms, one Bronto Aerial Tower Vehicle, three Emergency Medical Rescue Services units and seven water tankers.
They were assisted by 67 personnel from 11 volunteer fire brigades, as well as the police, Subang Jaya City Council personnel and the Selangor Wholesale Market security team.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Proton opens 10 outlets in 10 days
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Proton Holdings Bhd has expanded its network with the opening of 10 new Proton and Proton e.Mas outlets within 10 days, strengthening customer access to its products and aftersales services nationwide.
The expansion comprises four Proton outlets and six e.MAS outlets across the central, northern, southern and east coast regions as well as Sabah and Sarawak, supported by a combined dealer investment of RM15.9 million.
The new facilities are expected to create 210 jobs across sales, aftersales, technical and customer service functions.
As of June, Proton's nationwide network comprised 195 sales outlets, including 178 integrated 3S and 4S centres.
Its Proton e.Mas network consists of 58 dealerships, 45 service centres and 10 Body and Paint centres.
The new facilities include sales and customer centres, mall-based showrooms, integrated 3S and 4S outlets, an urban brand centre and a mini 3S outlet, allowing the company to tailor its retail presence to the needs of different markets.
Proton deputy chief executive officer Datuk Ir Abdul Rashid Musa said the 10 new outlets underscores the close collaboration between Proton, Proton e.Mas and its dealer partners.
He added that each outlet strengthens the car maker's relationship with customers, from the point of purchase through the entire ownership journey.
"Customer convenience is shaped by both proximity and the quality of support available. Our objective is not simply to add more locations, but to ensure each outlet responds to the needs of its local market and provides customers with a professional and dependable experience," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Going durian crazy at Grab's Kaw Kaw Fest
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Grab Malaysia will host its Durian Kaw Kaw Fest from July 25 to 26 at Elmina Lakeside Mall, featuring 35 booths and a series of activities highlighting its integrated lifestyle services.
Powered by Grab Dine Out, the festival will run from 4pm to 11pm daily.
The event aims to showcase the synergy within the Grab ecosystem, bringing together services such as Grab Dine Out, Grab Mart and the platform's strategic partnership with Jaya Grocer.
Grab Malaysia head of communications Daisy Sidhu said the selection of Elmina Lakeside Mall as the venue was strategic as it houses a Jaya Grocer outlet.
This allows the festival to demonstrate Grab's grocery delivery capabilities alongside its dine-in and takeaway offerings.
"The festival essentially demonstrates that durians are accessible through various channels on our platform, providing users with the flexibility to either dine out or enjoy the fruit at home," she said.
She said the event was curated for both those who enjoy the traditional dining experience and those who prefer the convenience of the app's delivery and takeaway services.
The 35 participating vendors include Grab platform merchants and local vendors offering fresh durians, as well as durian-based products such as crepes and ice cream.
The event will also feature appearances by local influencers.
Visitors using Grab Dine Out can access several promotions, including up to 30 per cent off fresh durians at selected merchants and 150 GrabCoins for every table booked through the app.
Users can also redeem one GrabCoin for an RM5 Dine Out voucher.
The festival also introduces an interactive element, where visitors can reserve game slots via the Grab app for a chance to win free durians.
Sidhu said the campaign, which was first launched in 2023, had evolved from its initial format.
It began as a promotion offering durians for RM1 to mark the launch of GrabPay and has since transitioned into a full-scale community festival.
"The initiative is about integrating the various offerings within our ecosystem into a cohesive experience.
"It is one of the many ways Grab continues to champion local vendors and small and medium enterprises, particularly in support of Visit Malaysia Year," she said.
Ahead of the event, representatives from Grab Malaysia visited the New Straits Times office to present a token of the "king of fruits" to Media Prima News and Current Affairs deputy managing director and New Straits Times group editor Farrah Naz Karim, and New Straits Times deputy group editor Sharanjit Singh.
The festival remains open to the public, inviting the community to celebrate the peak of the durian season.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Witness tells court of RM20.3 million transfers from PNB to FashionValet
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Sessions Court was told today that there were two fund transfers totalling RM20.3 million from Permodalan Nasional Bhd (PNB) to Fashion Valet Sdn Bhd.
Maybank Jalan Tun Razak branch assistant manager Shamsiah Musa, 55, said the two transactions were made on June 4, 2018, and Dec 20, 2018.
"I have reviewed all monthly account statements for 2018. I confirm that there were only two transfers from PNB's Malayan Banking Bhd account to Fashion Valet Sdn Bhd's Public Bank account," she said.
She was reading her witness statement on the second day of the trial involving Fashion Valet founder Datin Vivy Yusof and her husband Datuk Fadzarudin Shah Anuar, who are facing criminal breach of trust (CBT) charges involving RM8 million in investment funds from Khazanah Nasional Bhd and PNB.
Referring to PNB's account statements, the second prosecution witness confirmed that the transfers were for RM11,953,509 on June 4, 2018, and RM8,365,001 on Dec 20, 2018.
Shamsiah said she could not confirm any information or notes indicating the reason or purpose of the transfers as she did not have access to transactions beyond six months, and checks could only be made based on available bank statements.
When questioned by the couple's lawyer Datuk M. Athimulan about the transactions, Shamsiah said there were no freezes or restrictions imposed by PNB and confirmed that the transactions were valid and transparent.
Athimulan: When the bank received the funds from PNB, did PNB give any instructions that the use of the funds be frozen or restricted?
Shamsiah: No.
Athimulan: Based on bank records, all transactions relating to the PNB funds were processed lawfully, transparently, and in compliance with banking standards and procedures?
Shamsiah: Agreed.
The witness also agreed with the lawyer that she had no knowledge of or access to the terms of the agreement between PNB and Fashion Valet.
Vivy, whose full name is Vivy Sofinas Yusof, 38, and her husband Fadzarudin, 37, as directors of FashionValet Sdn Bhd, are charged with intentionally committing CBT involving investment funds from Khazanah and PNB.
The RM8 million payment was allegedly made from FashionValet's bank account to 30 Maple without the approval of the company's board of directors at Public Bank Bhd's Bukit Damansara branch on Aug 21, 2018.
They are charged under Section 409 of the Penal Code, which provides for
imprisonment of between two and 20 years, as well as caning and a possible fine upon conviction.
The couple also pleaded not guilty to an alternative charge of intentionally and dishonestly misappropriating FashionValet's property, namely the RM8 million from Khazanah and PNB, at the same location and on the same date.
The alternative charge was brought under Section 403 of the Penal Code, which carries imprisonment of between six months and five years, with caning and a fine upon conviction.
The trial before Judge Rosli Ahmad resumes on Aug 17.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Animal group seeks transparency in Bentong animal cruelty case
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Department of Veterinary Services has been urged to provide a transparent update on enforcement action taken over an animal neglect and cruelty case in Kampung Chemomoi, Bentong.
The Stray Animal Foundation Malaysia questioned whether the department had officially opened an investigation paper and identified those responsible for the alleged abuse.
This follows the circulation of photos and video footage showing emaciated, weak and severely neglected animals confined in filthy cages without adequate food or water. Reports also indicated that dead kittens and chickens were discovered at the premises.
"This is not animal care. The situation clearly demonstrates severe neglect and animal cruelty.
"Failing to provide sufficient food, water or shelter, as well as causing or allowing animals to suffer pain and distress, constitutes an offence under Section 29 of the Animal Welfare Act 2015," the foundation said in a statement yesterday.
The foundation posed a series of questions to the department regarding the status of its investigation.
"What are the results of the veterinary examinations on the animals found alive and dead? Has the individual who has custody or control of the animals been identified and their statement recorded?
"What enforcement actions have been and will be taken, including measures to protect other animals still at risk? If there is sufficient evidence, when will the investigation paper be referred to the Public Prosecutor for a decision on prosecution?" the foundation asked.
Under Section 29 of the Animal Welfare Act 2015, anyone convicted of animal cruelty faces a fine of between RM20,000 and RM100,000, imprisonment for up to three years, or both.
Veterinary Services personnel inspected the location on July 15 and rescued nine sick cats for medical treatment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Defence Ministry tightens grenade training safety after Hobart camp tragedy
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Defence Ministry and armed forces will tighten safety measures for grenade training after an investigation into last month's fatal explosion at Hobart Camp in Gurun, Kedah.
Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the measures would be implemented to reduce the risk of similar incidents recurring.
"Following these findings, the ministry and armed forces will strengthen safety procedures, grenade handling protocols, supervision levels and training implementation to reduce the risk of similar incidents occurring in the future," he said in a statement.
Khaled said that the safety of military personnel would remain the ministry's highest priority.
"There will be no compromise in ensuring that all training exercises are conducted with the highest level of compliance, supervision and safety," he said.
He said this following the completion of an investigation into an incident with the probe concluding that the grenade's safety lever was believed to have been released
before the throw
was completed, causing it to detonate while still near the two personnel.
In the incident, Corporal Norazmi Abu Bakar of the 6th Battalion Royal Malay Regiment and Private Siti Khadijah Sungip of the 1st Squadron Royal Engineers Regiment died during a grenade throwing exercise at the Hobart Camp firing range.
They were rushed to Sultan Abdul Halim Hospital in Sungai Petani after the 10.57am incident, but died on the way.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Selangor Wholesale Market redevelopment to include enhanced fire safety features
Article:
SERDANG: The Selangor Wholesale Market is expected to undergo redevelopment early next year, with enhanced safety features incorporated into its new design.
Selangor Infrastructure and Agriculture Committee chairman Datuk Izham Hashim said the redevelopment plan was being finalised, with construction expected to begin in 2027.
Izham, who is also the Selangor Infrastructure and Agriculture exco member, said safety would be a key priority in the new design, including improved firefighting systems, adequate water supply for firefighting purposes, sprinkler systems and sufficient parking facilities.
The redevelopment, he said, would also transform the facility into a more modern market, providing a cleaner and more comfortable environment for traders, businesses and visitors.
He said the current structure was outdated and needed to be upgraded to meet modern expectations.
"People often associate markets with unpleasant conditions, but modern markets can be developed into clean and comfortable spaces while still offering fresh produce at affordable prices.
"In the past, safety considerations may have been different based on the standards at that time. But for the new development, safety must be a priority," he said.
"We need to consider aspects such as fire prevention, user comfort and overall infrastructure," he added.
Izham said the redevelopment had been in the pipeline for some time, but implementation was delayed to address contractor concerns.
"We have resolved the matter and are now finalising the arrangements. We want people to look forward to coming here for the overall environment," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: ICC prosecutor Karim Khan's future hangs in the balance over sexual assault allegations
Article:
THE HAGUE: Karim Khan will go down in the history of the International Criminal Court as the prosecutor who clinched arrest warrants against the leaders of Russia and Israel but also as the one who "poisoned the atmosphere" within the institution.
Rigour and precision are the watchwords of this 56-year-old British lawyer when it comes to building a case against the alleged perpetrators of the world's most serious crimes.
But he now faces his own forensic investigation following allegations of sexual assault made against him by a member of his staff – accusations he denies.
At his swearing-in ceremony in 2021 as chief prosecutor of the Hague-based court, Khan said the ICC should be judged on its actions: "The proof of the pudding should be in the eating."
And by obtaining arrest warrants for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Hamas leaders, he has shown he does not shy away from controversy.
Before that, he won an arrest warrant for Russian President Vladimir Putin, which led to Khan being placed on a wanted list in Russia.
The official has courted controversy throughout a career that has included defending former Liberian president Charles Taylor against allegations of war crimes in Sierra Leone.
Other high-profile clients have included Kenyan President William Ruto who faced charges of crimes against humanity at the ICC – a case that was ultimately dropped.
He was also the lawyer for Seif al-Islam, son of the late Libyan leader Muammar Gaddafi.
Asked about "crossing the floor" – working as both prosecutor and defence – Khan told specialist journal OpinioJuris it helps lawyers stay "grounded."
It also prevents "corrosive traits such as thinking that defence counsel is the devil incarnate or that, as a prosecutor, you are doing 'God's work'," he added.
Initially criticised for dithering over atrocities in Gaza, Khan touched off a firestorm by applying for arrest warrants against Netanyahu and Hamas leaders.
Netanyahu called it a "moral outrage of historic proportions" while then US president Joe Biden described Khan's actions as "outrageous."
Even before Khan's applications, senior US Republicans had penned a letter threatening to bar him and his family from the United States, ending ominously "you have been warned."
In his defence, Khan stressed: "This is not a witch hunt... It is a forensic process that is expected of us as international prosecutors."
- 'AWARE OF HIS ACHIEVEMENTS' -
Born in Scotland, Karim Khan studied at a private school in northern Britain, before reading law at King's College London.
He cut his teeth in international law at the former Yugoslav and Rwandan war crimes tribunals from 1997 to 2000, before representing survivors and relatives of victims of Cambodia's 1970s Khmer Rouge regime before a UN-backed court.
Other roles include a stint at the Special Tribunal for Lebanon, based in The Hague, created to try the assassins of former Lebanese Prime Minister Rafic Hariri in 2005.
With a nod to the arrogance some critics ascribe to him, the ICC selection panel described him as a "charismatic and articulate communicator who is well aware of his achievements."
"I did apply because I thought I could do the role. If the Search Committee thought this was arrogance, then I'm guilty as charged," retorted Khan.
His speeches feature a strong command of rhetoric, peppered with touches of British humour.
A member of the minority Ahmadiyya Muslim sect, he often punctuates his speeches with "Inshallah" (God willing).
"Karim Khan seems like a no-nonsense lawyer," Melanie O'Brien, visiting professor in international law at the University of Minnesota, told AFP.
An ICC prosecutor "has to have a certain fortitude because you know you are going to be up against people who don't agree with you and who don't agree with the court in general," she added.
But whatever the outcome of Friday's vote on whether to sack him as prosecutor, the allegations will have tarnished his work at the ICC.
"Even if they are only accusations, it disturbs us and it has poisoned the atmosphere of the court," Deputy Prosecutor Mandiaye Niang told AFP in an interview in December.
The accusations "are out there and that is enough to cast a veil of doubt over what we're doing", he said.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Onn Hafiz defends Johor's 10 deputy exco appointments
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has defended the appointment of 10 deputy executive councillors, saying the move is lawful, administrative in nature and does not create new executive powers or alter the state's governing structure.
Responding to criticism from several Pakatan Harapan (PH) leaders, Onn Hafiz said the appointments were made within the state government's administrative powers and based on advice from the Johor State Legal Adviser.
He said the deputy exco members were not part of the state executive council and therefore did not possess any decision-making authority.
"They do not attend state executive council meetings and have no authority to approve policies, financial matters or any executive decisions.
"All decision-making powers remain fully vested in the State Executive Council as provided under the Johor Constitution," he said in a statement posted on Facebook.
He said the appointments neither created new executive authority nor transferred any powers from existing exco members.
Instead, he described them as an administrative mechanism aimed at strengthening policy implementation, project monitoring and public service delivery.
"This appointment does not alter the mandate given by the people through elections.
"It is intended to enhance the state's administrative capacity so that policies and services can be implemented more efficiently, quickly and effectively," he said.
He also dismissed claims that the move was intended to create additional political positions or expand the state bureaucracy.
Onn Hafiz said Johor's growing development agenda, including the Johor Economic Transformation Plan (JETP), Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ), Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link and other high-impact projects planned through 2030, required stronger administrative support to ensure effective implementation.
"As Johor continues to drive these strategic initiatives, the state's administrative capacity must also be strengthened so that every policy can be translated into tangible outcomes that benefit Bangsa Johor," he said.
The Machap assemblyman also assured that the appointments would have minimal financial implications, as they would utilise existing government assets and facilities without creating new portfolios or expanding the administrative structure.
He reiterated that the state government remained committed to transparent, prudent and responsible governance, adding that every decision was made based on the interests of the people, current needs and efforts to strengthen Johor's administrative capabilities.
The appointment of the 10 deputy exco members has drawn criticism from opposition parties, which questioned the purpose, cost and transparency of the newly created positions.
Muda president Amira Aisya Abd Aziz described the move as a waste of public funds, accusing the Barisan Nasional (BN)-led state government of using taxpayers' money for political consolidation.
Senai assemblyman Wong Bor Yang of DAP also called on the state government to provide a detailed explanation of the rationale behind the appointments, their scope of duties and the financial implications involved.
On July 22, Johor made history by appointing its first-ever deputy executive councillors.
Onn Hafiz unveiled the 10-member line-up, saying the appointments were aimed at strengthening the state's administrative machinery and accelerating the implementation of government policies.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MACC officer rejects claim Penang tunnel land deal caused no loss
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The investigating officer in the Penang undersea tunnel corruption trial today rejected a defence claim that the state government suffered no losses after transferring state land to pay for the project's feasibility study.
Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) senior investigating officer Zulhilmi Ramli told the Sessions Court he disagreed with the suggestion that the land swap arrangement did not result in any loss to the state government.
Zulhilmi, the 38th prosecution witness, said this during cross-examination by lawyer Mohd Haijan Omar in Lim Guan Eng's corruption trial before Sessions Court judge Azura Alwi.
He said the transfer of state-owned Lot 702 was made to settle a RM135 million claim by Consortium Zenith Beijing Urban Construction Group (CZBUCG) for work on the feasibility study.
CZBUCG was awarded the RM6.34 billion Penang undersea tunnel and major roads project.
Haijan asked Zulhilmi to confirm that CZBUCG's final claim under the land swap arrangement for Lot 702 amounted to RM138,971,572.61.
Haijan:
The state government has already paid for the work carried out by CZBUCG through a RM135 million land swap. Do you agree?
Zulhilmi:
I agree.
Haijan:
Based on your testimony, the work had already been carried out, certified by HSSI Integrated Sdn Bhd (the independent technical consultant), and Lot 702 could therefore be used as payment. So the state government did not suffer any loss from transferring Lot 702?
Zulhilmi:
I disagree.
Lim is charged with using his position as chief minister to obtain RM3.3 million in gratification by helping a company owned by former CZBUCG director Datuk Zarul Ahmad Mohd Zulkifli secure the RM6.34 billion Penang undersea tunnel and major roads project between January 2011 and August 2017.
The Bagan member of parliament also faces a charge of soliciting 10 per cent of the profits Zarul was expected to earn from the project as an inducement for helping the company secure the contract.
He is further charged with causing two plots of state government land, valued at RM208.8 million, to be disposed of to a developer linked to the undersea tunnel project.
Earlier in the trial, Zulhilmi testified that on Oct 6, 2013, the state government, represented by Lim, signed a preliminary agreement with Zarul for the construction of three major roads and the undersea tunnel.
He said CZBUCG subsequently appointed a company to undertake the feasibility study before proceeding with the construction works.
Under the preliminary agreement, the feasibility study was valued at RM305.88 million, with payment to be made through the transfer of part of either Lot 702 or Lot 713 in Bandar Tanjung Pinang.
The hearing continues.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Local logistics firms squeezed by Chinese rivals' aggressive pricing
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Local logistics companies are facing mounting pressure from Chinese competitors offering what Kenanga Research described as "irrational pricing", even as operating costs continue to rise.
The research house said aggressive pricing by Chinese logistics firms is limiting the ability of local players, including Swift Haulage Bhd, to benefit fully from Malaysia's robust trade growth.
It attributed the pricing pressure to Chinese operators maintaining low rates despite rising manpower costs and tighter vehicle weight limit regulations.
Kenanga Research said many Chinese logistics firms enter Malaysia alongside Chinese foreign direct investments, particularly when new manufacturing plants are established.
Despite the competition, it remains constructive on the domestic logistics industry, supported by Malaysia's expanding e-commerce market and trade diversion arising from ongoing US-China trade tensions.
Malaysia's e-commerce gross merchandise value (GMV) is projected to grow at a compound annual growth rate of five per cent from 2024 to 2027, reaching RM1.5 trillion by 2027 from RM1.2 trillion in 2024.
The firm said the growth will boost demand for distribution hubs and warehouses to support just-in-time deliveries, while also encouraging reshoring and nearshoring activities.
It expects greater adoption of warehouse automation, customer system integration and decentralised warehousing to improve supply chain resilience and reduce transportation costs.
"There is also strong demand for cold-storage warehouses on the back of the proliferation of online grocery start-ups," it said.
Kenanga Research maintained its "Neutral" call on the seaports and logistics sector but upgraded Bintulu Port Holdings Bhd to "Outperform" with a target price of RM7.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Three shops affected by Selangor Wholesale Market fire, others remain open
Article:
SERDANG: Businesses unaffected by the fire at the Selangor Wholesale Market have continued operating as usual after the blaze damaged part of the market last night.
Selangor Infrastructure and Agriculture Committee chairman Datuk Izham Hashim said the incident only involved three premises, while other traders at the market continued their operations.
"The fire involved three shops - one selling catering equipment, another selling eggs and a hardware shop.
"However, other shops are operating as normal and business activities at unaffected premises have not been disrupted," he told reporters during a visit to the affected site today.
He said the Selangor Agricultural Development Corporation (PKPS), as the market operator, would continue monitoring the situation and work with relevant agencies following the incident.
Earlier, Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ashrul Riezal Asbar said the
cause of the fire at the market
was expected to be determined within 14 days.
He said forensic investigations would begin once the site was declared safe and cleared of debris.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Marine police bust international drug syndicate, intercept RM6.1 million haul
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Marine police have crippled an international drug smuggling attempt after intercepting a fibre boat carrying drugs worth RM6.1 million in waters off Pulau Rimau here.
The operation saw the seizure of 113.7kg of suspected methamphetamine valued at RM5.68 million and 3,497 vape cartridges containing liquid suspected to be laced with methamphetamine, weighing 6.3kg and worth RM315,000.
Bukit Aman Internal Security and Public Order Department director Datuk Seri Mohd Yusri Hassan Basri said the success was the result of intelligence-led enforcement by the Marine Police Region One Penang during Op Gelora 190/2026 on July 17.
He said the operation was launched after the Marine Police intelligence unit detected suspicious movements involving a fibre boat believed to have been used by a drug trafficking syndicate to smuggle narcotics through Malaysian waters.
"During a patrol, the team detected a suspicious fibre boat about seven nautical miles south of Pulau Rimau.
"When ordered to stop for inspection, the suspects accelerated and carried out dangerous manoeuvres in an attempt to escape.
"However, the Marine Police personnel managed to intercept the boat following a chase at sea before further checks were conducted," he told a press conference at the marine base in Batu Uban here today.
Present were state police chief Datuk Dennis Lim and Marine Police commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Shamsol Kassim.
Yusri said the inspection revealed that the drugs were hidden in a specially modified compartment located at the front section of the boat.
"The drugs were packed inside sacks and concealed in the compartment, believed to be an attempt to evade detection by enforcement authorities," he said.
He said the boat, believed to have been used as transportation by the syndicate, was also seized, bringing the overall value of the confiscated items to RM6.1 million.
The seizure, he said, had prevented a significant quantity of drugs from reaching the international market, with the methamphetamine alone estimated to have the potential to be abused by more than 570,000 people.
"The seizure also disrupted the supply chain of an international drug syndicate and potentially saved thousands of lives from the impact of drug abuse," he said.
Two Indonesian men, aged 30 and 51, were arrested during the operation and are believed to have acted as sea transporters tasked with moving the drug consignment from Malaysia to Indonesia.
Yusri said both suspects had been remanded for seven days to assist investigations, with the case being investigated under Section 39B of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952. The offence carries the death penalty or imprisonment for life, as well as whipping upon conviction.
He said preliminary investigations found that the boat involved was locally registered, while the origin of the drug supply remained under investigation by the Penang Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department (NCID).
"The supply source of the drugs is still being investigated and the matter has been handed over to the Penang NCID for further investigation," he said.
Yusri added that the suspects were believed to have received payment of RM10,000 each for their role as transporters, but details of the amount involved would be determined through investigations.
He said the latest seizure highlighted the continued efforts by drug syndicates to exploit maritime routes as alternative channels for cross-border smuggling.
"Police, through the Marine Police, will continue strengthening intelligence gathering, patrols and enforcement operations to ensure any attempt by cross-border criminal syndicates is detected and stopped at an early stage," he said.
Up to June this year, Op Taring Gelora conducted by Marine Police Region One Penang recorded 337 cases, nine arrests and seizures worth RM226,000.
For overall Marine Police Region One Penang operations during the same period, a total of 361 cases were recorded, involving 49 arrests and seizures amounting to RM10.4 million.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: South Korean AI chip billionaire ordered to pay US$644m to ex-wife in divorce
Article:
SEOUL: A South Korean court ordered an AI chip-manufacturing tycoon on Friday to make a trimmed-down US$644 million divorce payment to his former wife.
Chey Tae-won, 65, is the chairman of the SK Group, which owns world-leading memory chipmaker SK hynix – a pillar of South Korea's tech-led economy.
Seoul High Court in 2024 had told him to pay 1.38 trillion won (US$940 million) to his ex-wife Roh Soh-yeong, daughter of a former South Korea president.
The sum – equal to more than US$1 billion at the exchange rate at the time of the ruling – led local media to dub it the "divorce of the century."
Chey appealed to the Supreme Court, which told the high court to reconsider its 2024 decision and led to the tycoon's reduced divorce bill of 944 billion won (US$644 million).
The settlement is not yet final, however, as either side may refer it back to the Supreme Court.
The acrimonious case centres on the definition and valuation of the couple's shared assets, including Chey's lucrative stake in the SK Group.
Seoul High Court ruled Friday that Roh was entitled to a cash payout worth one-third of the couple's marital assets.
"The defendant's shareholdings increased substantially during the marriage through his management activities," the court said.
"The plaintiff contributed to that increase through homemaking, childcare and public-facing activities related to SK Group."
Chey and Roh married in 1988, but the couple are believed to have lived apart for more than 15 years. In 2015 he admitted to fathering a child with his lover.
With SK hynix selling vital memory chips to AI computing company Nvidia, Chey's stake is now worth roughly US$5.5 billion, according to regulatory filings and public data.
Roh, 65, has three children with Chey and has built a career in the cultural space, running a digital art museum.
- Bribes -
The pair were wed in Seoul's presidential Blue House when her father Roh Tae-woo led the country from 1988 to 1993.
They have battled over the split in the courts since 2022, after Chey filed for divorce in 2017 and mediation broke down.
The court's original 2024 ruling ordering the payout to Roh cited a 30 billion won slush fund her ex-president father used to propel SK Group's growth.
The court said at the time that could be counted as her contribution towards the couple's shared assets.
But in October last year the Supreme Court quashed that argument, saying that any such cash likely stemmed from illegal bribes and therefore should not be counted.
Another question was whether the dramatic increase in SK Group's value since the couple's separation, on the cusp of the AI boom, should be reflected in the asset division.
"The court took into account the sharp rise in the value of the defendant's shareholdings when determining the property division ratio," Friday's court statement said.
SK Group shares have more than tripled in value since December 2015, when Chey publicly disclosed his extramarital affair.
The Supreme Court had already separately ordered Chey to make an alimony payment to Roh of 2 billion won.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BN-PN pact will not change coalition's multiracial stance, says Asyraf
Article:
KUALA PILAH: Barisan Nasional's (BN) understanding with Perikatan Nasional (PN) in the Negri Sembilan election will not change the coalition's commitment to protecting and serving all Malaysians, said Umno secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki.
He said BN remained a coalition dedicated to safeguarding the welfare of all Malaysians, regardless of race or religion.
"The Chinese and Indian communities need not fear this understanding because BN has been a multiracial coalition since its inception.
"We are accustomed to managing a multiracial and multireligious country, and BN will remain that way," he said in his a speech at the Ibol District Polling Centre in Seri Menanti here, alongside BN candidate for the Seri Menanti state seat, Muhammad Sufian Maradzi.
He said since BN's formation, its component parties - Umno, MCA and MIC - had proven that racial diversity could be managed harmoniously.
He said the coalition continued to respect religious and cultural diversity, including recognising major festive celebrations of every community.
"This homeland belongs to everyone; it belongs to the Malays, as well as the Chinese and Indians.
"What is vital is that all parties respect the Federal Constitution, the position of Islam as the religion of the Federation, the special rights of Malays and Bumiputeras, and the rights of other races," he said.
He said the understanding between BN and PN required both sides to set aside past differences to build greater collective strength.
He said Umno's leadership decided to cooperate with PN after listening to grassroots calls for Malay political unity.
"If we want to bring up what Pas did to us, it will never end. If Pas wants to bring up what Umno did to them, it will never end either.
"That is past history. We are looking forward because Muslims long for this unity and cooperation," he said.
In the spirit of the understanding, Asyraf said BN had made "sacrifices" by ceding several of its traditional seats to PN to improve their chances of forming the state government.
He added that PN had not imposed any conditions regarding state executive appointments should the coalition successfully form the state government.
Asyraf also hinted that the BN-PN cooperation in Negri Sembilan could serve as a catalyst for broader political collaboration in the future.
"It looks like Negri Sembilan could be the start of a much larger movement," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Selangor Sultan attends Yasin recital in conjunction with Warriors' Day
Article:
SHAH ALAM: The Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, today attended the 'yasin' recital, 'doa selamat' prayers and 'tahlil' at the Sultan Salahuddin Abdul Aziz Shah Mosque here, in conjunction with the state-level Warriors Day celebration.
The event, which was led by Selangor Deputy Mufti Datuk Dr Miszairi Sitiris, was also attended by Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari; Islamic Affairs and Innovation Committee chairman Dr Mohammad Fahmi Ngah; Youth, Sports and Entrepreneurship Committee chairman Mohd Najwan Halimi and Selangor Islamic Religious Department (Jais) director Datuk Mohd Shahzihan Ahmad.
About 1,100 officers and personnel from the security forces and uniformed agencies serving in Selangor were among those who attended the event.
The congregation then performed the Friday prayers led by Sultan Salahuddin Abdul Aziz Shah Mosque Grand Imam Datuk Muhammad Farhan Wijaya, who delivered a sermon titled 'Hakikat Kepahlawanan Islam'.
Warriors' Day is celebrated on July 31 each year to honour the struggles and sacrifices of the members of the national security forces to protect the country's sovereignty. —BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: China opposes new US tariffs, warns against trade wars
Article:
BEIJING: Beijing condemned fresh US tariffs imposed on China and 59 other countries over alleged forced labour concerns Friday, and warned Washington against waging a trade war.
The US levies range from 10 to 12.5 per cent, with China slapped with the highest rate.
"We oppose all forms of unilateral tariff measures," Chinese foreign ministry spokesperson Lin Jian told a news briefing on Friday.
"Tariff wars and trade wars are not in the interests of any party," he warned.
The new tariffs replace an expiring global duty rolled out by President Donald Trump earlier this year.
The Trump administration has moved swiftly to rebuild the president's tariff wall after the Supreme Court struck down a host of his duties in February – dealing a blow to his ability to unleash steep levies at will.
In unveiling the duties, US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said Washington was "rigorously" enforcing a forced labour import ban and that it was "well past time for our trading partners to do the same."
Economies that have implemented a forced labour prohibition – including Canada, the European Union and the United Kingdom – received the lower 10-per cent rate.
Other major trade partners including India and Japan were hit with the higher 12.5-per cent tariffs.
China and the United States, which spent much of last year embroiled in an escalating trade war, reached a truce when Trump and President Xi Jinping met last October.
After Trump visited Beijing this year, China said it would work with the United States on reducing tariffs, but the latest levies threaten to renew trade tensions.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Court dismisses RM110mil suit by China businessman over 51 luxury units
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A businessman from China who bought 51 luxury residential units has lost his RM110 million lawsuit against the developer and marketing company after claiming he had been misled into purchasing the properties for hotel-style accommodation use.
High Court judge Datuk Anand Ponnudurai dismissed Cheah Ting Feng's suit against Lumayan Indah Sdn Bhd and 1 Pavilion Property Consultancy Sdn Bhd, ruling that he had failed to prove he was induced by any fraudulent or negligent misrepresentation.
The court also found that Cheah had failed to establish any breach of contract or illegality that would justify rescinding the sale and purchase agreements (SPAs) for the units at Banyan Tree Signatures in Jalan Conlay.
Cheah had sought to rescind all 51 SPAs executed in April 2019, recover the RM110 million purchase price and obtain damages, alleging he had been promised the units could be operated as hotel or short-term rental accommodation with guaranteed rental returns.
However, the judge found there was "a complete and total absence" of evidence to support those allegations.
"There were no emails, WhatsApp messages, WeChat exchanges, brochures, promotional materials or other documents showing the defendants had promised guaranteed rental returns or hotel-style operations.
"Instead, the plaintiff himself admitted during cross-examination that there was nothing in any marketing material recording such promises," he said in his grounds of judgment dated July 10.
Anand said this was particularly significant given that the transaction involved the purchase of 51 units worth RM110,000,040 after negotiations spanning about five months.
"It defies commercial reality and common sense that no written record whatsoever would exist to evidence such a representation or promise," he said.
The court also accepted the evidence of the defendants' sales and marketing witness, who categorically denied promising guaranteed rental returns or telling the plaintiff he could operate the units as a hotel or begin short-term rental operations upon completion.
The judge said the plaintiff was a seasoned businessman who negotiated the transaction over five months and signed the SPAs together with the Deeds of Mutual Covenant (DMCs), which expressly restricted the units to residential use.
"It is commercially inconceivable that a seasoned businessman would have been induced to invest the substantial sum of RM110 million based solely on oral representations while signing multiple documents that contradicted those alleged representations," he said.
The court further held that the SPAs contained an entire agreement clause expressly superseding all prior oral and written negotiations, while the DMCs clearly stated that the units "shall only be used for residential purposes and for no other purpose".
On the plaintiff's claim that the units were built in breach of Kuala Lumpur City Hall's approved building plans, the court found the issue had effectively become academic.
The judge also rejected the argument that the alleged deviations from the approved building plans rendered the SPAs void for illegality.
The court held that the object of the contracts was simply the lawful sale and purchase of residential units, and that any regulatory non-compliance under the Street, Drainage and Building Act did not automatically invalidate the agreements.
The court made no order as to costs.
Cheah was represented by S.K. Ramesh and Lorelle Nathan, while Joshua Teoh, Gooi Yang Shuh and Valerie Chung appeared for the defendants.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Honda Malaysia powers up Melaka plant with solar
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Honda Malaysia Sdn Bhd has completed its first solar power installation project at its manufacturing plant in Melaka, marking a big step in its sustainability journey and long-term goal of achieving carbon neutrality by 2050.
The project, which was commemorated by Melaka Chief Minister Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh, involved about RM14 million investment.
The company said the initiative reflects its commitment to integrating sustainable practices into its manufacturing operations while supporting Malaysia's renewable energy agenda.
The solar power system consists of about 8,960 solar panels installed across the rooftops of the Melaka plant, with a total generation capacity of more than 5.6 megawatts.
The company expects the system to generate about 7,042 megawatt-hours of renewable electricity annually, reducing the plant's carbon dioxide emissions by around 4,584 tonnes a year, equivalent to about 10 per cent of its total carbon emissions.
Honda Malaysia managing director and chief executive officer Narushi Yazaki said the project represents an important milestone in the company's efforts to reduce its environmental footprint.
"Beyond reducing our environmental footprint, it reflects our commitment to responsible manufacturing and our aspiration to contribute towards a carbon-neutral future," he added.
In addition to the solar photovoltaic system, Honda Malaysia has also installed a battery energy storage system, which stores excess solar energy for later use and helps ensure a more stable electricity supply for operations.
Yazaki said the company aims to expand its solar power initiative to its parts supply warehouse in Bukit Jelutong, Shah Alam, in 2027 as part of its broader sustainability strategy.
The solar project reinforces Honda Malaysia's commitment to sustainable business growth while supporting Honda's global target of achieving carbon neutrality across all products and corporate activities by 2050.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar: Another Petronas tanker set to arrive in Malaysia next month
Article:
PORT DICKSON: Another Petronas tanker that has been granted clearance to transit the Strait of Hormuz is expected to arrive in Melaka early next month, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said.
He said Petroliam Nasional Bhd, president and group chief executive officer, Tengku Tan Sri Muhammad Taufik had informed him of the latest development yesterday.
Anwar said the vessel's successful passage reflected Malaysia's prudent foreign policy, particularly its ability to maintain good diplomatic relations with Iran.
"Another Petronas vessel departed from Yanbu, Saudi Arabia, crossed the Red Sea, was granted clearance and will arrive in Melaka in early August.
"Alhamdulillah. This shows that our foreign policy is prudent," he said in his speech at the Semarak Kenegaraan programme at the Army Basic Training Centre here.
Also present were Deputy Defence Minister Adly Zahari, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar and Armed Forces chief General Tan Sri Malek Razak Sulaiman.
Anwar said Malaysia's good ties with countries, including the United States and Gulf nations, had enabled it to engage effectively in diplomatic negotiations when issues arose.
He recalled that when a Petronas vessel was delayed following the US-Iran conflict several months ago, Malaysia's strong bilateral relations enabled him to speak directly with Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian.
"After expressing my sympathies, I asked him to allow the Petronas vessel to proceed.
"One of the first vessels granted clearance to transit the Strait of Hormuz was the Petronas tanker, which has since arrived in Pengerang," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ukraine hits 'Russia's Amazon' warehouses outside St Petersburg
Article:
SAINT PETERSBURG: Ukrainian drones Friday hit warehouses for Russian e-commerce giant Wildberries for a third time in six days, this time near Saint Petersburg, triggering a fire that sent smoke pouring over the historic city.
Kyiv has accused Wildberries – Russia's largest online retailer often known as the Russian equivalent of Amazon – of storing drone parts and helping the Russian army.
Ukraine has targeted Saint Petersburg as it steps up long-range drone strikes on Russian energy, military and logistics sites in recent weeks, calling the attacks justified retribution for Moscow's nightly drone and missile barrage of its cities.
Russia's defence ministry said its forces shot down some 571 drones overnight over more than a dozen regions and Crimea, the Black Sea peninsula that Russia annexed in 2014 – one of the largest one-night barrages of the war.
In Saint Petersburg, an AFP reporter saw grey smoke rising into the morning sky over high-rise residential buildings in the city, President Vladimir Putin's hometown that is more than 800km from the Ukrainian border.
Wildberries said two of its logistics facilities in Saint Petersburg and one in Simferopol, in Moscow-annexed Crimea, were hit.
"Rapid evacuation was carried out at all the warehouses ... according to preliminary data, there are no casualties," Wildberries CEO Tatyana Kim said on social media.
Alexander Drozdenko, governor of the surrounding Leningrad region  said three people were wounded in the attack, which saw at least 59 drones fired towards Saint Petersburg.
It was the third overnight attack by Ukraine on Wildberries' sites in the past week.
Last weekend, eight night-shift workers were killed in attacks on Wildberries sites that saw one of the company's main warehouses outside Moscow burn to the ground.
Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy alleged after the first attack that the facilities were used to "supply sanctioned components for drone production and navigation equipment."
Hundreds of small businesses saw their inventories wiped out in the strike, triggering angry comments on social media.
Kyiv calls its strikes "long-range sanctions", saying the attacks are justified retribution for Russia's more than four-year offensive and nightly attacks on its cities.
The campaign has triggered nationwide fuel shortages and, Kyiv says, dented Russia's financial war chest.
- 'ESCALATION' -
Columns of thick black smoke were still billowing into the sky from one of the factories hours after the hit, an AFP reporter saw.
Russian President Vladimir Putin spent the first years of the war trying to shield the population from the fall-out of his decision to launch the offensive on Ukraine.
But the strikes on oil refineries have triggered fuel shortages and big attacks near major cities have brought the dramatic visible consequences of the war into the homes of many Russians.
Firefighters and investigators had been deployed to the sites in Saint Petersburg as the blaze raged through the morning, Russian officials said.
Saint Petersburg's Pulkovo airport suspended flights during the attack, resuming operations around 6am (0300 GMT), with around 50 departures disrupted.
Both Russia and Ukraine have escalated their long-range strikes in recent months.
Moscow's forces have fired increasing numbers of ballistic missiles at Kyiv – killing dozens and ripping open apartment blocks.
US President Donald Trump has appeared to give his backing to Ukraine's counter-attacks on Russia – calling the strikes "an  escalation that can lead to an end."
Washington's attempts to mediate a peace deal between the warring sides have largely stalled with its own war on Iran raging.
The United Nations has reported a surge in civilian deaths in 2026, saying June was the deadliest month since mid-2022.
Hundreds of thousands of soldiers and tens of thousands of civilians have been killed since Russia launched its full-scale offensive in February 2022 – though there is no precise toll with neither side routinely revealing the number of their losses.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia remains in lower US tariff tier
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia remains among the 17 economies subject to the US' lower 10 per cent Section 301 tariff following Washington's investigations into forced labour-related import enforcement across 60 economies.
The tariff, which took effect on July 24, follows the expiry of the temporary tariff imposed under Section 122 of the US Trade Act of 1974.
The Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry today said Malaysia's placement in the lower tariff tier reflects the United States Trade Representative's (USTR) recognition of the country's commitments to implement and enforce laws prohibiting the importation of goods produced using forced labour.
The remaining economies covered by the investigation are subject to a higher 12.5 per cent tariff rate, the ministry said in a statement.
The ministry also said the USTR's Section 301 investigation into excess capacity, involving 16 economies including Malaysia, remains ongoing.
The ministry said it would update stakeholders once the USTR determines any tariff measures arising from the excess capacity investigation, adding that it will continue engaging with the US on the matter.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Thousands flee wildfires in southern Europe
Article:
LÈGE-CAP-FERRET, France: Luggage-clad residents and tourists fled a French peninsula by boat and the smell of smoke drifted into parts of Madrid on Friday as wildfires swept through parts of western Europe, triggering states of emergencies and calls for EU help.
In France, fires raging near the country's prized wine country of Bordeaux forced hundreds of people to flee the Cap Ferret peninsula in the middle of the summer vacation season as the blaze devoured an area nearly twice the size of Manhattan.
In Spain, two separate blazes were raging out of control within 100km of Madrid, with some residents of the Spanish capital waking up to the smell of smoke, according to residents.
"The gendarmes came and knocked on every door, the fire was about 500 metres away – we grabbed some things and left," Patrick Martineau, a 69-year-old resident of Le Porge, south of Bordeaux, said.
Three firefighters have been killed as southern Europe endures more severe heat, with temperatures topping 40C in some areas.
The total area of land burnt so far this year in EU countries is the second largest on record, according to satellite data from the European Forest Fire Information System (EFFIS).
French authorities have ordered thousands of tourists on the southwestern Atlantic coast out of campsites and holiday homes, as firefighters backed by water-bombing planes raced to control the blazes.
More than 23,000 people have been evacuated in the region so far.
France has invoked the European Union's civil protection mechanism to ask for help, President Emmanuel Macron said on Friday, warning that the crisis remained "very intense."
The EU said it would provide three water-bombing planes to France.
In Spain, the government declared a state of emergency in the Madrid region, where more than 10,000 people have been evacuated in recent days, with Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez calling the situation "dramatic."
"Meteorological conditions, fundamentally the wind, will complicate firefighting operations this Friday," the Madrid regional government said in a post on X.
France's southeast was also being hard hit, with dozens of fires flaring up and being fanned by the mistral wind around the historic village of Cotignac – officials saying around 25 houses had been destroyed since Tuesday.
In Italy, thousands of emergency workers battled scores of fires in Sicily and in the Calabria region, where a local official said arsonists had started fires by tying rags soaked in flammable liquid to the tails of stray cats to spread the flames.
One firefighter died after falling ill while battling the flames in Sicily, the interior minister said.
Two firefighters died on Tuesday tackling another blaze near Bordeaux airport.
- 'NO CHANCE' -
Skies over Le Porge turned orange as smoke rose from charred and crackling pine trees, while helicopters made repeated runs over deserted neighbourhoods.
Fires in 2022 damaged a massive area of the same forest, forcing around 50,000 people to flee.
The blazes are breaking out faster because of climate change caused by the burning of oil, coal and gas. European droughts are now more severe, climatologists from the World Weather Attribution group said in a study published on Thursday.
In Spain, hundreds of firefighters and troops were still struggling to contain a huge wildfire in Guadalajara province, north of Madrid and a smaller fire near the city of Toledo, which forced the evacuation of several villages.
"Climate change is what kills, what destroys our villages and our natural and cultural heritage," civil protection chief Virginia Barcones told Spanish radio.
She warned some fires become so intense that, despite the deployment of aircraft and ground crews, firefighters stand "no chance."--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: CIMB anchors Malaysia's US$1.5bil global sukuk
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: CIMB Group Holdings Bhd has strengthened its debt capital markets position as the sole Malaysian joint lead manager and joint bookrunner for the government's US$1.5 billion global sukuk issuance.
The bank worked alongside HSBC, JP Morgan and Standard Chartered on the transaction, which set a new benchmark yield curve for future Malaysian corporate issuances in the international debt capital markets.
CIMB said its appointment reflected the company's strong domestic debt capital markets franchise, global distribution capabilities and leadership in Islamic finance.
The bank supported investor engagement and bookbuilding efforts through its network of institutional investors across Asia, the Middle East, Europe and other international markets.
It also leverages its market intelligence and execution expertise to help secure broad global investor participation.
Group chief executive officer Novan Amirudin said the bank's role reflects the strength of its debt capital markets platform, global distribution capabilities and longstanding leadership in Islamic finance.
"This transaction demonstrates our ability to originate, structure and distribute benchmark sukuk transactions to a diverse international investor base.
"By combining our domestic market leadership with deep engagement across global fixed income investors, we were able to contribute meaningfully to the successful execution of the issuance and further strengthen Malaysia's standing in the international Islamic capital markets," he said.
The issuance attracted strong demand from global institutional investors and achieved the tightest spread ever for a Malaysian government US dollar sukuk.
The offering was 4.7 times oversubscribed, with the peak order book exceeding US$9.5 billion.
CIMB said the pricing reflected Malaysia's strong credit standing and investor confidence, with spreads comparable to those achieved by AA-rated sovereign peers.
The bank added that the transaction sets a new benchmark, achieving both funding and investor diversification objectives while attracting broad-based investor participation, underscoring strong confidence in the country's robust economic fundamentals.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: How TikTok is becoming Malaysia’s newsroom — Marissa Mae
Article:
July 24 — Ten years ago, most Malaysians got their news from the morning paper or the evening television bulletin.
Today, millions are more likely to discover breaking news while scrolling through a 60-second TikTok video on their For You Page.
According to the Reuters Institute’s Digital News Report, TikTok has become the fastest-growing platform for news consumption worldwide.
Malaysia is not just following this global trend but is emerging as one of its leading examples.
In 2024, around 31 per cent of Malaysians said they used TikTok to access news.
By 2025, that number had surged to 48 per cent, placing Malaysia among the world’s highest users of TikTok for news alongside Thailand.
Globally, the Reuters Institute found that one in three people now use TikTok, with around half of those users turning to the platform for news.
Among people aged 18 to 24, social media has overtaken traditional news outlets, with 44 per cent saying it is now their main source of news.
The shift reflects a generation that increasingly values speed, accessibility, and visual storytelling over lengthy articles.
A one-minute video can explain a complex political issue before a reader has even finished the first paragraph of a traditional news report.
News is no longer something people actively search for, but something that finds them while they scroll.
This changing habit is transforming not only how audiences consume information but also how journalists produce and deliver it.
The author argues that TikTok’s rapid rise as a news source in Malaysia is reshaping how audiences consume information and how journalists produce and deliver news. — AFP pic
The rise of TikTok has also introduced “newsfluencers”, creators who summarise and explain current events for audiences that rarely watch television or read newspapers.
Many of these creators have built loyal communities by making complicated issues feel easier to understand and more relatable.
However, the growing influence of newsfluencers has also raised concerns about accuracy, bias, and the spread of misinformation.
Unlike professional news organisations, many independent creators are not bound by editorial standards or formal fact-checking processes.
As a result, opinions, rumours, and incomplete information can sometimes spread as quickly as verified news.
Recognising these challenges, Malaysia’s Online Safety Act 2025 will bring platforms such as TikTok and Instagram under broader local regulations beginning in January 2026.
For young Malaysians, TikTok is no longer just a platform for entertainment, viral dances, or comedy skits.
It has become a daily source of updates on politics, the economy, education, and issues that directly affect their lives.
This shift reflects a wider digital transformation, where social media is becoming one of society’s primary gateways to information.
The trend also mirrors what many researchers are beginning to observe, with students increasingly relying on social media not only for communication and learning but also to stay informed about current affairs.
As digital platforms continue to shape everyday life, adapting to this new media environment is becoming less of a choice and more of a necessity.
While some remain sceptical about getting news through social media, the reality is that digital platforms are rapidly becoming the places where younger generations discover, discuss, and share information.
The real question is no longer whether social media should be part of the news ecosystem, but whether society is prepared to navigate it responsibly.
Media literacy is therefore becoming one of the most important skills young people can develop in the digital age.
As more Malaysians turn to their For You Page for the day’s headlines, the challenge is no longer teaching people how to find news, but how to recognise what is truly worth believing.
* This is the personal opinion of the writer or publication and does not necessarily represent the views of
Malay Mail
.

Title: ‘I have made mistakes, but do not slander me’: PM Anwar hits out at false claims during Negeri Sembilan campaign
Article:
PORT DICKSON, July 24 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged all parties to refrain from spreading slander and false information for political gain, particularly during the campaign for the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election.
Anwar said he had himself been the target of false claims, including allegations that he had convened a meeting to spread propaganda among serving military personnel and veterans while campaigning for the election.
“This morning, one of my officers showed me a message claiming, ‘Anwar called people together to spread propaganda among the military.’ I was stunned. Politics has come to this. They simply cut and pasted things before presenting them as my statement.
“I am not perfect, and I have many weaknesses. I have made mistakes in my life and, if I have said or done anything wrong, I apologise. But do not slander me,” he said during the Semarak Kenegaraan programme at the Army Basic Training Centre (Pusasda) today.
Also present were Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, Deputy Defence Minister Adly Zahari, Chief of Defence Forces General Tan Sri Malek Razak Sulaiman and Army chief General Tan Sri Azhan Md Othman.
Anwar also reflected on the many false accusations and unfair treatment he had endured throughout his life, expressing hope that others would learn from those experiences and refrain from using slander as a means of achieving political success.
He also called on the civil service to continue supporting the government in safeguarding national peace and stability, while ensuring its reform agenda and policy changes are implemented effectively.
In the Negeri Sembilan state election, Pakatan Harapan (PH) is contesting all 36 state seats, fielding 16 PKR candidates, 11 from DAP and nine from Amanah.
Early voting is set for July 28, with polling day on August 1. For the latest updates on the state polls, visit
https://prn.bernama.com/n9
. — Bernama

Title: PNB imposed no restrictions on RM20.3m transferred to FashionValet, witness tells court
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 24 — The Sessions Court was told today that there were two fund transfers totalling RM20.3 million from Permodalan Nasional Berhad (PNB) to Fashion Valet Sdn Bhd.
Maybank Jalan Tun Razak branch assistant manager Shamsiah Musa, 55, said the two transactions were made on June 4, 2018, and December 20, 2018.
“I have reviewed all monthly account statements for 2018. I confirm that there were only two transfers from PNB’s Malayan Banking Berhad account to Fashion Valet Sdn Bhd’s Public Bank account,” she said.
